results.query: DOGE Department of Government Efficiency instability fallout Q1 2026
results.description: DOGE faced opposition, lawsuits, and criticisms warning of constitutional crises and coup-like actions during Q1 2026, with ongoing legal challenges over its access to sensitive data and authority without congressional input. Its status remained unclear as the temporary organization is scheduled to end on July 4, 2026, amid disputes over savings claims and costs. No evidence of outright instability or fallout like collapse was found.

results.query: Executive Order 14375 legal challenge International Organizations Immunities Act 2026
results.description: Executive Order 14375, issued January 22, 2026, designates the Board of Peace as a public international organization entitled to privileges and immunities under the International Organizations Immunities Act (IOIA).[5][6] No search results mention any legal challenge to EO 14375 or its IOIA authorization as of March 11, 2026. results.query: NTEU National Treasury Employees Union court order DOGE position list disclosure 2026
results.description: No evidence found of a court order on February 27, 2026, requiring the National Treasury Employees Union (NTEU) to disclose a position list related to DOGE. Search results detail ongoing NTEU litigation over executive orders, RIF plans, FOIA requests for position reclassifications, and union contract terminations, but none mention a court-ordered disclosure of positions by NTEU involving DOGE on that date. results.query: Schedule Policy Career federal employee reclassification implementation March 2026
results.description: The Schedule Policy/Career rule took effect on March 9, 2026, as scheduled. The final rule was published in the Federal Register on February 5-6, 2026, and became effective 30 days later. However, actual reclassification of positions into the new category is ongoing—agencies have submitted lists of positions to the White House for presidential review, with President Trump to make final decisions on which positions are moved into Schedule Policy/Career.

Israel issued a 60-day ultimatum to Hamas to disarm on February 16, 2026, with the period possibly starting around a US Board of Peace meeting on February 19, 2026, as stated by Cabinet Secretary Yossi Fuchs. Hamas officials rejected the ultimatum, stating they had not been notified and calling it baseless threats without basis in negotiations. As of March 11, 2026, approximately 24 days into the potential 60-day period, no resolution or compliance was reported.

Iran launched multiple missile attack waves at Israel on March 6-7, 2026, and continued drone and missile strikes on regional countries including Saudi Arabia and the UAE. A US-Israeli coalition conducted extensive airstrikes on Iranian military targets, oil facilities, and missile production sites since late February 2026, significantly degrading Iran's capabilities. The conflict remained active as of March 7, with signals of potential de-escalation discussed on March 10.
